mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2-year moving average trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in above-trend GDP quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0% signals labor market normalization that reduces recession probabilities and supports consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 6.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized construction and employment expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC above 0.05, or individual series show stronger predictive power than the interaction term in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, each 25 basis point increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates faster than near-term earnings can adjust, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive Fed fund rate hikes over a 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% over a rolling three-month window confirms supply-side capacity utilization, reducing input cost bottlenecks and supporting corporate margins, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds the 1.5% threshold across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales revenue combined with stable consumer price indices indicate real purchasing power growth without inflationary pressure, which sustains equity earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows negative or zero predictive power, or inflation spikes simultaneously with sales growth and returns turn negative in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 jobs consistently validates underlying economic momentum, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC drops below 0.01, or forward returns show no directional bias when payroll growth exceeds the threshold across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near the zero lower bound, equity markets price in prolonged liquidity support and capital flight into risk assets, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or statistically insignificant when the policy rate stays below 0.25% for consecutive months, indicating liquidity preference dominates.
